data.name: Subtitle_FfbOutputCurve
data.value: // drag the 5 nodes to shape response

data.name: Subtitle_HighSpeedDamping
data.value: // I have no idea what these settings do

data.name: Hint_LedButtonSettingsInDeviceTab
data.value: LED and button settings are in the MOZA Wheel device tab under Devices.

data.name: Subtitle_MapPhysicalPullToGame
data.value: // map physical pull to game input

data.name: Subtitle_MapPhysicalPositionToGame
data.value: // map physical position to game input

data.name: Subtitle_MBoosterPedals
data.value: // select role per connected mBooster pedal

data.name: Hint_MBoosterDisplayName
data.value: Optional — helps tell two mBoosters with the same role apart in the device list above.

data.name: Subtitle_PedalFeel
data.value: // shape how the pedal feels, independent of the output curve below

data.name: Hint_OutputCurveDragX
data.value: Drag a node to move where it sits on the input axis

data.name: Hint_BrakeFadeExperimental
data.value: Experimental — writes the real Travel End and Max Threshold hardware calibrations while active (more travel and more force needed), then restores your configured values as brakes cool. Requires those already set in Pedal Feel / Sim Input Mapping — has no effect otherwise.

data.name: Subtitle_CustomEffectsExperimental
data.value: // experimental — user-defined vibration driven by SimHub/NCalc formulas

data.name: Hint_CustomEffectsExperimental
data.value: Experimental — each custom effect vibrates the motor using a SimHub property or NCalc formula you supply. All custom effects (and the built-in Engine effect) share one wire channel, so only one can drive the motor at a time; the most recently processed active effect wins. Frequency/Intensity behave like Engine's own sliders.

data.name: Hint_CustomEffectFormula
data.value: A SimHub property (e.g. SpeedKmh) or NCalc formula (e.g. [Rpms] / [CarSettings_MaxRPM]). With "Use Threshold" off, the value (clamped 0..1) scales Intensity continuously. With it on, the effect pulses at the configured Intensity whenever the value is at or above Threshold.

data.name: Subtitle_CalibrationExperimental
data.value: // experimental — may not be honored by mBooster firmware

data.name: Hint_LedOptionsRecommendedOff
data.value: Recommended: leave LED options below OFF unless you experience LED issues.

data.name: Hint_OnlySendLedWhenChanged
data.value: Only send LED data when it actually changes.

data.name: Hint_AlwaysResendBitmask
data.value: Always send the LED bitmask with color updates, even if unchanged. Causes extra traffic.

data.name: Hint_DisableSerialProbe
data.value: Only required if plugin is talking to incorrect device.

data.name: Option_FirmwareEraAuto
data.value: Auto — Attempt to detect from wheel handshake

data.name: Hint_FirmwareEra
data.value: Auto detects the wheel's firmware era from its handshake and picks the matching protocol. Override only if telemetry does not work.

data.name: Hint_ClearAllSettingsWarning
data.value: This will reset all plugin settings and profiles to their defaults.

data.name: Hint_DashboardUploadNotWorking
data.value: Dashboard upload is in development and does NOT currently push files to the wheel.

data.name: Subtitle_DashboardUpload
data.value: // push a .mzdash file to the connected wheel

data.name: Hint_UploadStatusFormat
data.value: Status reflects the latest wheel ack.

data.name: Hint_UploadRequiresConnection
data.value: Note: Uploads require the wheel to be connected and a management session negotiated.

data.name: Subtitle_CoapServer
data.value: // enable this option to prevent PitHouse from launching automatically

data.name: Hint_CoapServerEnabled
data.value: Provides official MOZA SDK support for games that require it — such as iRacing, or any game that uses 360 Hz mode. The plugin runs an embedded CoAP SDK control server on loopback.

data.name: Hint_CoapStubProcessName
data.value: While enabled, the plugin runs a small stand-in process that appears in your process list as "MOZA Pit House.exe". This is expected — it lets MOZA SDK apps find PitHouse by name. It exits automatically when you disable this or close SimHub.

data.name: Hint_UdpControlEnabled
data.value: This is a legacy UDP control for setting steering angle. The only known use is the RSF version of Richard Burns Rally.

data.name: Hint_AboutDescription
data.value: An unofficial SimHub plugin that talks directly to MOZA Racing hardware over serial — hardware configuration, LED effects pipeline, and dashboard streaming. Not affiliated with MOZA / Gudsen Technology.

data.name: Hint_SponsorshipsAppreciated
data.value: Sponsorships are greatly appreciated, adding support for hardware I do not own can be difficult!

data.name: Hint_ThanksToTesters
data.value: Huge thanks to all the early alpha and beta testers who put their time — and their sanity — on the line while I figured out how to make the MOZA protocol work. This plugin wouldn't exist without you.

data.name: Banner_SdkSuggestionText
data.value: If you want to prevent MOZA Pit House from launching, enable SDK support.

data.name: Status_InstalledRestartRequired
data.value: Update installed — restart SimHub to load v{0}

data.name: Status_InstallFailedPendingRestart
data.value: previous install pending — restart SimHub first

data.name: Hint_CaptureDataInMemory
data.value: Data lives in memory only — nothing is saved between launches. Click Stop to Export a ZIP bundle for diagnostic support.

data.name: Label_AlwaysCaptureOnStartup
data.value: Always start diagnostics capture on launch/game switch

data.name: Tooltip_AlwaysCaptureOnStartup
data.value: When on, the serial traffic capture begins automatically each time the plugin starts.

data.name: Hint_ClickExpandToRender
data.value: Click expand to render the full multi-section report.

data.name: Hint_FullDiagRendered
data.value: // {0} lines · same content as Export bundle

data.name: Hint_MBoosterIntro
data.value: Each connected mBooster appears below. Pick a role (Throttle/Brake/Clutch) per device to route its pedal position. The Calibration section is experimental.

data.name: Hint_NoBaseAmbientDetected
data.value: No base ambient LEDs detected. R9/R12 wheelbases don't ship the LED strip; this panel only activates on R21/R25/R27-class bases.

data.name: Hint_NoJoystickConfig
data.value: No joystick configuration reported by the wheel yet. Connect the wheel and wait for the identity probe to complete.

data.name: Tooltip_LoadMzdash
data.value: Load a local .mzdash file for tier-def channel matching

data.name: Tooltip_SetFolder
data.value: Set a folder of .mzdash files as a dashboard library

data.name: Tooltip_AutoDetect
data.value: Auto-detect the dashboard folder for the connected wheel

data.name: Hint_ClickPencilForProperty
data.value: Pencil to pick a property, or the formula button for an NCalc / js: expression

data.name: Subtitle_RpmLedColorsStatic
data.value: // shown when telemetry is not sending · click a LED to recolor

data.name: Subtitle_ButtonLedColors
data.value: // click a button to recolor · 'default during telemetry' replaces off-buttons with the chosen color

data.name: Tooltip_KnobDefaultDuringTelemetry
data.value: When SimHub drives the knob LEDs fully off, restore the wheel's stored knob colors instead of going dark.

data.name: Tooltip_KnobStaticTimeout
data.value: Restore native knob colors after the telemetry color has been held unchanged this long. 0 = off.

data.name: Subtitle_KnobColours
data.value: // click any ring LED, or the centre swatch for that knob's active colour

data.name: Subtitle_SleepLight
data.value: // what the wheel shows after the idle timeout elapses

data.name: Hint_LanguageChangeRestart
data.value: Close and re-open this settings page to apply the new language. Auto follows SimHub's language setting, then the OS.

data.name: Import_NoFolderFound
data.value: PitHouse Presets folder not found. Set a custom path or use Browse.

data.name: Import_ConfirmHeader
data.value: Applying "{0}" to profile "{1}". The following settings will change:

data.name: Import_NoChangesHeader
data.value: All values in this preset already match your active profile.

data.name: Import_BrowseDescription
data.value: Pick a PitHouse preset JSON file from anywhere on your system.

data.name: Import_Error_UnsupportedType
data.value: Unsupported preset type "{0}". Only Motor and Pedals presets are supported.

data.name: Status_TelemetryParked
data.value: Telemetry stopped after repeated failures — re-enable to retry

data.name: Status_DegradedScreenless
data.value: Wheel has no display — telemetry not needed

data.name: Banner_PortLocked_Title
data.value: MOZA hardware detected but the port is in use

data.name: Banner_PortLocked_Body
data.value: Found a MOZA device on {0}, but the port is held by another application. Close MOZA PitHouse (or anything else talking to the wheelbase) and click Refresh.

data.name: Banner_PortVanished_Body
data.value: The MOZA device on {0} stopped responding to open. Replug the wheelbase (or its USB cable) and click Refresh.

data.name: Banner_TelemetryDegraded_Body
data.value: This is expected for wheels without a screen — no action needed.

data.name: Banner_TelemetryParked_Body
data.value: Toggle dashboard telemetry off and on to retry.

data.name: Banner_RestartSimHub_Body
data.value: New device definitions were deployed. Restart SimHub to add them under Devices.

data.name: Banner_ProfileNotAddedDash_Body
data.value: A MOZA dashboard is connected, but the device hasn't been added in SimHub. Open Devices > Add device > {0}.

data.name: Banner_ProfileNotAddedBaseAmbient_Body
data.value: This wheelbase has an ambient LED strip, but '{0}' hasn't been added in SimHub. Open Devices > Add device > {0} to drive the strip.

data.name: Banner_ProfileNotAddedWheel_Body
data.value: Wheel '{1}' is connected, but '{0}' hasn't been added in SimHub. Open Devices > Add device > {0}.

data.name: Sdk_ServerNotStarted
data.value: Server not started — enable with the toggle above

data.name: Sdk_NoRequestsYet_Udp
data.value: No requests yet — third-party tools only talk here when they read or write a setting

data.name: Status_CapturingClickStop
data.value: capturing… (always-capture is on — click Stop when ready)

data.name: Status_CapturingOpenTab
data.value: capturing… (open another tab and use the device, then come back to stop)

data.name: Status_PickMzdashFirst
data.value: Pick a .mzdash file or library entry first.

data.name: Upload_CannotResolveBytes
data.value: Cannot resolve raw bytes for "{0}" — pick a local file instead.

data.name: Upload_Queued
data.value: Upload queued — pushing "{0}" to the wheel…

data.name: Upload_NotStarted
data.value: Upload not started — wheel not connected or no management session yet.

data.name: Upload_AutoDetect_NotFound
Upload_AutoDetect_NoFolderForWheel
data.value:
No dashboard folder for the connected wheel (UID {0}). Looked under: {1}
Open a dashboard in MOZA Pit House for this wheel first.

data.name: Upload_AutoDetect_NoFolders
data.value: No wheel-specific dashboard folders found under _dashes. Open MOZA Pit House and load a dashboard, then try again.

data.name: Upload_AutoDetect_Multiple
data.value: Multiple dashboard folders found ({0}) and no wheel is connected. Connect your wheel and try again, or use Set Folder… to choose manually.

data.name: Dialog_ClearAllSettings_Body
data.value:
This will permanently delete all plugin settings and profiles.
Are you sure?

data.name: Dialog_CannotDeleteNoId
data.value: Cannot delete "{0}": wheel did not assign an id.

data.name: Dialog_ConfirmDelete_Body
Dialog_CompletelyRemoveTimeout
data.value: completelyRemove("{0}") timed out. The dashboard may still be present on the wheel.

data.name: Import_NoMotorPresets
data.value: // no Motor presets in this folder

data.name: Import_NoPedalsPresets
data.value: // no Pedals presets in this folder

data.name: Import_Footer_NoMappable
data.value: no mappable settings in this preset

data.name: Import_Footer_AllMatch
data.value: all {0} settings already match — nothing to apply

data.name: Dialog_RestartWheelbase_Body
data.value: Restart the wheelbase now? Force feedback will drop briefly while the base reboots.

data.name: Hint_PaddleCalibrate
data.value: Hold all paddles fully, then release. Saving in {0}s…

data.name: Hint_CalibratePedal
data.value: Press the pedal through its full range, then release. Saving in {0}s…

data.name: Hint_CalibrateHandbrake
data.value: Pull the handbrake fully, then release. Saving in {0}s…

data.name: Hint_KeepaliveTimeout
data.value: How long to keep refreshing the LEDs after telemetry stops before the wheel can sleep. 0 = stop immediately.

data.name: Hint_StalksTruckSim
data.value: In truck-sim mode the plugin turns stalk presses into keyboard keys for Euro/American Truck Simulator. In button-box mode the stalks act as plain buttons.

data.name: Subtitle_StalksInteractiveMap
data.value: Press a stalk control to highlight its button, then assign a key or wiper/light stage.

data.name: Subtitle_StalksWiperSync
data.value: The game steps wipers/lights with cycle keys; the plugin sends the right number of presses to reach the stalk position.

data.name: Hint_ResyncWipers
data.value: Drives the game wipers back to off to re-align with the plugin's tracked stage.

data.name: Hint_MBoosterAxisRoles
data.value: This mBooster hosts more than one pedal on one connection. Assign each detected axis to a game input. The default order follows Moza's Rx/Ry/Rz layout (throttle/brake/clutch) — remap here if a pedal drives the wrong input.

data.name: Note_PassivePedalNoEffects
data.value: This pedal is passive (no motor) — vibration effects don't apply.
